1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: WOE TO THOSE who give short measure,

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Ruin is for the defrauders. (Those who measure less.)

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Woe to Al-Mutaffifin [those who give less in measure and weight (decrease the rights of others)],

1.author: Qarai
1.text: Woe to the defrauders, who use short measures,

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: Woe to those who give less [than due],

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: Woe to those who are fraudulent in (weighing and measuring),

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: Woe to those who give short measure,

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: Woe to those that deal in fraud,-

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: Who insist on being given full when they take from others,

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: Those who when they take the measure from others, take it in full!

2.author: Arberry
2.text: when they measure against the people, take full measure

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: Those who, when they take by measure from mankind, exact the full,

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: Those who, when they have to receive by measure from men, demand full measure,

2.author: Itani
2.text: Those who, when they take a measure from people, they take in full.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: those who, when they take from others by measure, take their full share;

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: Those who, when they have to receive by measure from men, demand full measure,

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: Those who when they take the measure from mankind demand it full,

2.author: Qarai
2.text: who, when they measure [a commodity bought] from the people, take the full measure,

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: who, when people measure for them, take full measure,

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: Who, when they take a measure from people, take in full.

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: those who demand a full measure from others

2.author: Shakir
2.text: Who, when they take the measure (of their dues) from men take it fully,

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: who demand of other people full measure for themselves,

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: Those who, when they have to receive by measure from men, exact full measure,

2.author: Transliteration
2.text: Allatheena itha iktaloo AAala alnnasi yastawfoona

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: Whilst when they measure or weigh for them, give less.

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: Whereas when they give others after measuring or weighing, they give them less!

3.author: Arberry
3.text: but, when they measure for them or weigh for them, do skimp.

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: And who, when they measure Unto them or weigh for them, diminish.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: And when they have to give by measure or weight to men, give less than due.

3.author: Itani
3.text: But when they measure or weigh to others, they cheat.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: but who, when they measure or weigh for others, give less than their due.

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: And when they have to give by measure or weight to men, give less than due.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: But if they measure unto them or weight for them, they cause them loss.

3.author: Qarai
3.text: but diminish when they measure or weigh for them.

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: but when they measure or weigh for others, they reduce!

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: But if they give by measure or by weight to them, they cause loss.

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: but when they measure or weigh, give less.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: But when they measure out to others or weigh out for them, they are deficient.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: but when they give by measurement or weight to others, they give them less.

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: But when they have to give by measure or weight to men, give less than due.

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: Do they not think they will be raised (to life) again

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: What! Do they not expect that they will be raised?

4.author: Arberry
4.text: Do those not think that they shall be raised up

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: Imagine such men not that they shall be raised up?

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: Think they not that they will be resurrected (for reckoning),

4.author: Itani
4.text: Do these not know that they will be resurrected?

4.author: Maududi
4.text: Do they not realise that they will be raised to life

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: Do they not think that they will be resurrected,

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: Do such (men) not consider that they will be raised again

4.author: Qarai
4.text: Do they not know that they will be resurrected

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: Do they not think that they will be resurrected

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: Do they not think that they will be resurrected

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: Do they not realize that they will be resurrected

4.author: Shakir
4.text: Do not these think that they shall be raised again

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: Do such people not realize that they will be raised up,

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: Do they not think that they will be called to account?-

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: (To be raised) for a Great Day?

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: The day all mankind will stand before the Lord of all the worlds?

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: The day when everyone will stand before the Lord Of The Creation.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: a day when mankind shall stand before the Lord of all Being?

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: A Day whereon mankind shall stand before the Lord of the worlds?

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: The Day when (all) mankind will stand before the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists)?

6.author: Itani
6.text: The Day when mankind will stand before the Lord of the Worlds?

6.author: Maududi
6.text: a Day when mankind will stand before the Lord of the Universe?

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: The Day when (all) mankind will stand before the Lord of all that exists

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: The day when (all) mankind stand before the Lord of the Worlds?

6.author: Qarai
6.text: a day when mankind will stand before the Lord of all the worlds?

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: the Day when people will stand before the Lord of the Worlds?

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: The Day when mankind will stand before the Lord of the worlds?

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: when mankind will stand before the Lord of the Universe?

6.author: Shakir
6.text: The day on which men shall stand before the Lord of the worlds?

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: The Day when mankind will stand before the Lord of the Universe?

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: A Day when (all) mankind will stand before the Lord of the Worlds?

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: Indeed the ledger of the wicked will be in (the lowest depths) Sijjin.

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: Indeed, the record of the disbelievers is in the lowest place, the Sijjeen.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: No indeed; the Book of the libertines is in Sijjin;

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: By no means! Verily the record of the ungodly is in Sijjin.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: Nay! Truly, the Record (writing of the deeds) of the Fujjar (disbelievers, sinners, evil-doers and wicked) is (preserved) in Sijjin.

7.author: Itani
7.text: Not at all. The record of the wicked is in Sijjeen.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: No indeed! Verily the deeds of the wicked are in the Record locked up in the prison-house!

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: Nay! Truly, the Record of the wicked is in Sijjin.

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: Nay, but the record of the vile is in Sijjin -

7.author: Qarai
7.text: Indeed, the record of the vicious is in Sijjeen.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: No indeed, the Book of the immoral is in Sijjeen.

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: No! Indeed, the record of the wicked is in sijjeen.

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: Woe to them! Let them know that the records of the sinner's deeds are in Sijin.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: Nay! most surely the record of the wicked is in the Sijjin.

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: Indeed! The record of the wicked is in the Sijjin --

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: Nay! Surely the record of the wicked is (preserved) in Sijjin.

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Kalla inna kitaba alfujjari lafee sijjeenin

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: How will you comprehend what Sijjin is?

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: And what do you know, how (wretched) the Sijjeen is!

8.author: Arberry
8.text: and what shall teach thee what is Sijjin?

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: And what will make thee know whatsoever the record in Sijjin is?

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: And what will make you know what Sijjin is?

8.author: Itani
8.text: But how can you know what Sijjeen is?

8.author: Maududi
8.text: And how would you know what the Record of the prison-house is?

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: And what will make you know what Sijjin is

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: Ah! what will convey unto thee what Sijjin is! -

8.author: Qarai
8.text: And what will show you what is Sijjeen?

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: What could let you know what the Sijjeen is!

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: And what can make you know what is sijjeen?

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: Would that you knew what Sijin is!?

8.author: Shakir
8.text: And what will make you know what the Sijjin is?

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: and what could make you understand what the Sijjin is? --

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: And what will explain to thee what Sijjin is?

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: It is a (repository of) distinctly written record.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: The record is a sealed text.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: It is [their destination recorded in] a register inscribed.

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: It is a comprehensively written Book (of records).

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: Ah the woe that day for those who deny,

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: Ruin is for the deniers on that day!

10.author: Arberry
10.text: Woe that day unto those who cry it lies,

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: Woe on that Day unto the belviers-

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: Woe, that Day, to those who deny [(Allah, His Angels, His Books, His Messengers, the Day of Resurrection, and Al-Qadar (Divine Preordainments)].

10.author: Itani
10.text: Woe on that Day to the deniers.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: Woe, then, to those that give the lie,

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: Woe, that Day, to those who deny.

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: Woe unto the repudiators on that day!

10.author: Qarai
10.text: Woe to the deniers on that day,

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: Woe on that Day to those who belied it,

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: Woe, that Day, to the deniers,

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: Woe, on that day, to those who have rejected God's revelations

10.author: Shakir
10.text: Woe on that day to the rejecters,

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: Woe, on that Day, to those who reject,

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: Woe, that Day, to those that deny-

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: Who call the Day of judgement a lie!

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: Those who deny the Day of Justice.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: who cry lies to the Day of Doom;

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: Those who belie the Day of Requital.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: Those who deny the Day of Recompense.

11.author: Itani
11.text: Those who deny the Day of Reckoning.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: those that give the lie to the Day of Recompense.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: Those who deny the Day of Recompense.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Those who deny the Day of Judgment

11.author: Qarai
11.text: who deny the Day of Retribution;

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: who belied the Day of Recompense!

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: Who deny the Day of Recompense.

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: and those who have rejected the Day of Judgment.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: Who give the lie to the day of judgment.

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: those who deny the Day of Judgement.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: Those that deny the Day of Judgment.

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: None denies it but the sinful transgressors.

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: And none will deny it, except every transgressor, rebel.

12.author: Arberry
12.text: and none cries lies to it but every guilty aggressor.

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: And none belieth it save each trespasser, sinner.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: And none can deny it except every transgressor beyond bounds, (in disbelief, oppression and disobedience of Allah, the sinner!)

12.author: Itani
12.text: But none denies it except the sinful aggressor.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: Yet none gives the lie to it except the transgressor immersed in sin;

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: And none can deny it except every transgressor beyond bounds, the sinner!

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: Which none denieth save each criminal transgressor,

12.author: Qarai
12.text: and none denies it except every sinful transgressor.

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: None belies it except every guilty sinner.

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: And none deny it except every sinful transgressor.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: No one rejects it except the sinful transgressors

12.author: Shakir
12.text: And none gives the lie to it but every exceeder of limits, sinful one

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: No one denies it except for the evil aggressor.

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: And none can deny it but the Transgressor beyond bounds the Sinner!

12.author: Transliteration
12.text: Wama yukaththibu bihi illa kullu muAAtadin atheemin

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: When Our revelations are recited before him, he says: "These are fables of long ago."

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: When you recite Our verses to him, he says, “Stories of earlier men!”

13.author: Arberry
13.text: When our signs are recited to him, he says, 'Fairy-tales of the ancients!'

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And when Our revelations are rehearsed Unto him, she saith:'fables of the ancients!

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: When Our Verses (of the Quran) are recited to him he says: "Tales of the ancients!"

13.author: Itani
13.text: When Our revelations are recited to him, he says, “Legends of the ancients.”

13.author: Maududi
13.text: who, when Our verses are recited to him, says: “Mere tales of olden times!”

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: When Our Ayat are recited to him, he says: "Tales of the ancients!"

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: Who, when thou readest unto him Our revelations, saith: (Mere) fables of the men of old.

13.author: Qarai
13.text: When Our signs are recited to him, he says, ‘Myths of the ancients!’

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: When Our verses are recited to him, he says: 'Fictitious tales of the ancients'

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: who, when listening to Our revelations, say, "These are only ancient legends".

13.author: Shakir
13.text: When Our communications are recited to him, he says: Stories of those of yore.

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: When Our revelations are conveyed to him, he says, "Fables of the ancients!"

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: When Our Signs are rehearsed to him, he says, "Tales of the ancients!"

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: Itha tutla AAalayhi ayatuna qala asateeru alawwaleena

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: No. In fact what they have been doing has rusted their hearts.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: Not at all – but rather their earnings have heaped rust upon their hearts.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: No indeed; but that they were earning has rusted upon their hearts.

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: By no means!' Aye! encrusted upon their hearts is that which they have been earning.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: Nay! But on their hearts is the Ran (covering of sins and evil deeds) which they used to earn.

14.author: Itani
14.text: Not at all. Their hearts have become corroded by what they used to earn.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: No indeed! The truth is that their hearts have become rusted on account of their evil deeds.

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: Nay! But on their hearts is the Ran (covering) which they used to earn.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: Nay, but that which they have earned is rust upon their hearts.

14.author: Qarai
14.text: No, that is not the case! Rather, their hearts have been sullied by what they have been earning.

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: No indeed! Their own deeds have cast a veil over their hearts.

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: No! Rather, the stain has covered their hearts of that which they were earning.

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: They will never have faith. In fact, their hearts are stained from their deeds.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: Nay! rather, what they used to do has become like rust upon their hearts.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: No! Their own deeds have cast a veil over their hearts.

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: By no means! but on their hearts is the stain of the (ill) which they do!

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Kalla bal rana AAala quloobihim ma kanoo yaksiboona

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: Therefore they will be screened off from their Lord that day,

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: Yes indeed – they will be deprived of seeing their Lord on that day.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: No indeed; but upon that day they shall be veiled from their Lord,

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: By no means! Verily on that Day from their Lord they will be shut Out.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: Nay! Surely, they (evil-doers) will be veiled from seeing their Lord that Day.

15.author: Itani
15.text: Not at all. On that Day, they will be screened from their Lord.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: No indeed! On that Day they will be screened off from seeing their Lord,

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: Nay! Surely, they will be veiled from seeing their Lord that Day.

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: Nay, but surely on that day they will be covered from (the mercy of) their Lord.

15.author: Qarai
15.text: Indeed, they will be alienated from their Lord on that day.

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: No indeed, on that Day they shall be veiled from their Lord.

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: No! Indeed, from their Lord, that Day, they will be partitioned.

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: On the Day of Judgment, they will certainly be barred from the mercy of their Lord.

15.author: Shakir
15.text: Nay! most surely they shall on that day be debarred from their Lord.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: Indeed! On that Day a barrier will be set between them and their Lord,

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: Verily, from (the Light of) their Lord, that Day, will they be veiled.

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Kalla innahum AAan rabbihim yawmaithin lamahjooboona

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: Then they will indeed burn in Hell.

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: Then indeed they have to enter hell.

16.author: Arberry
16.text: then they shall roast in Hell.

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: Then verily they will be roasted into the Scorch.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: Then, verily they will indeed enter and taste the burning flame of Hell.

16.author: Itani
16.text: Then they will roast in Hell.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: and then they shall enter Hell,

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: Then verily, they will indeed enter the burning flame of Hell.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: Then lo! they verily will burn in hell,

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: Then they shall roast in Hell,

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: Then indeed, they will [enter and] burn in Hellfire.

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: They will suffer the heat of fire

16.author: Shakir
16.text: Then most surely they shall enter the burning fire.

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: then they shall enter the Fire of Hell,

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: Further, they will enter the Fire of Hell.

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: They will then be told: "This is what you had denied."

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: It will then be said, “This is what you used to deny.”

17.author: Arberry
17.text: Then it shall be said to them, 'This is that you cried lies to.'

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Then it will be said: 'this is that which ye were wont to belie.'

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: Then, it will be said to them: "This is what you used to deny!"

17.author: Itani
17.text: Then it will be said, “This is what you used to deny.”

17.author: Maududi
17.text: whereafter they will be told: “This is what you used to give the lie to.”

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: Then, it will be said to them: "This is what you used to deny!"

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: And it will be said (unto them): This is that which ye used to deny.

17.author: Qarai
17.text: then told, ‘This is what you used to deny!’

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: and it will be said to them: 'This is that which you belied'

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: Then it will be said [to them], "This is what you used to deny."

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: and who will be told, "This is what you had called a lie".

17.author: Shakir
17.text: Then shall it be said: This is what you gave the lie to.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: and they will be told, "This is what you were wont to belie."

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: Further, it will be said to them: "This is the (reality) which ye rejected as false!

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Thumma yuqalu hatha allathee kuntum bihi tukaththiboona

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: Surely the ledger of the pious will be in 'Illiyun (heights above the heights).

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: Indeed the record of the virtuous is in the highest place, the Illiyin.

18.author: Arberry
18.text: No indeed; the book of the pious is in Illiyun;

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: By no means! Verily the record of the virtuous shall be in 'Illiyun.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: Nay! Verily, the Record (writing of the deeds) of Al-Abrar (the pious who fear Allah and avoid evil), is (preserved) in 'Illiyyun.

18.author: Itani
18.text: No indeed; the record of the righteous is in Elliyyeen.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: No indeed! Verily, the deeds of the virtuous shall be in the record of the exalted ones.

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: Nay! Verily, the Record of Al-Abrar (the righteous believers) is (preserved) in `Illiyyin.

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: Nay, but the record of the righteous is in 'Illiyin -

18.author: Qarai
18.text: Indeed, the record of the pious is in Illeeyun.

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: But, the Book of the righteous is in the 'Illiyoon.

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: No! Indeed, the record of the righteous is in 'illiyyun.

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: However, the records of the deeds of the virtuous ones will certainly be in Illiyin.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: Nay! Most surely the record of the righteous shall be in the Iliyin.

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: But, the record of the righteous is [preserved] in the 'Illiyyin --

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: Nay, verily the record of the Righteous is (preserved) in 'Illiyin.

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Kalla inna kitaba alabrari lafee AAilliyyeena

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: But how will you comprehend what 'Illiyun is?

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: And what do you know what the Illiyoon are!

19.author: Arberry
19.text: and what shall teach thee what is Illiyun?

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: And what will make thee know whatsoever the record in Illiyun is?

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: And what will make you know what 'Illiyyun is?

19.author: Itani
19.text: But how can you know what Elliyyoon is?

19.author: Maududi
19.text: And what do you know what the Record of the exalted ones is?

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: And what will make you know what `Illiyyin is

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: Ah, what will convey unto thee what 'Illiyin is! -

19.author: Qarai
19.text: And what will show you what is Illeeyun?

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: What could let you know what the 'Illiyoon is!

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: And what can make you know what is 'illiyyun?

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: Would that you knew what Illiyin is!

19.author: Shakir
19.text: And what will make you know what the highest Iliyin is?

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: and what will make you understand what the 'Illiyyin is? --

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: And what will explain to thee what 'Illiyun is?

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: It is a (repository of) distinctly written record

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: The record is a sealed text.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: A record of good deeds written.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: It is [their destination recorded in] a register inscribed

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: It is a comprehensively written Book (of records).

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: Witnessed by those who are honoured.

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: Which is witnessed by the close ones.

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: To which bear witness those brought nigh.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: To which bear witness those nearest (to Allah, i.e. the angels).

21.author: Maududi
21.text: which the angels placed near Allah to safeguard.

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: To which bear witness those nearest.

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: Attested by those who are brought near (unto their Lord).

21.author: Qarai
21.text: witnessed by those brought near [to Allah].

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: witnessed by those who are near (to Allah).

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: Which is witnessed by those brought near [to Allah].

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: The ones nearest to God will bring it to public.

21.author: Shakir
21.text: Those who are drawn near (to Allah) shall witness it.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: which those angels closest to God will bear witness to.

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: To which bear witness those Nearest (to Allah).

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: Verily the pious will be in heaven,

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: Indeed the virtuous are in serenity.

22.author: Arberry
22.text: Surely the pious shall be in bliss,

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: Verily the virtuous shall be in Delight,

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: Verily, Al-Abrar (the pious who fear Allah and avoid evil) will be in delight (Paradise).

22.author: Itani
22.text: Indeed, the righteous will be amid bliss.

22.author: Maududi
22.text: Verily the virtuous shall be in Bliss;

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: Verily, Al-Abrar (the righteous believers) will be in Delight.

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: Lo! the righteous verily are in delight,

22.author: Qarai
22.text: The pious shall be amid bliss,

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: The righteous shall indeed be blissful,

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: Indeed, the righteous will be in pleasure

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: The virtuous will live in bliss,

22.author: Shakir
22.text: Most surely the righteous shall be in bliss,

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: The virtuous will surely be in bliss,

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: Truly the Righteous will be in Bliss:

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: reclining on couches, reviewing (the bounties given to them).

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: seated on couches and gazing around in wonder.

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: On Thrones (of Dignity) will they command a sight (of all things):

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: On their faces you will see the glow of beatitude.

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: You will recognise the freshness of serenity on their faces.

24.author: Arberry
24.text: thou knowest in their faces the radiancy of bliss

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: Thou wilt recognize in their faces the brightness of delight.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: You will recognise in their faces the brightness of delight.

24.author: Itani
24.text: You will recognize on their faces the radiance of bliss.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: You shall see upon their faces the glow of bliss.

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: You will recognize in their faces the brightness of delight.

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: Thou wilt know in their faces the radiance of delight.

24.author: Qarai
24.text: You will perceive in their faces the freshness of bliss.

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: and in their faces you shall know the radiance of bliss.

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: You will recognize in their faces the radiance of pleasure.

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: You can trace on their faces the joy of their bliss.

24.author: Shakir
24.text: You will recognize in their faces the brightness of bliss.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: You will find in their faces the brightness of bliss.

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: Thou wilt recognise in their faces the beaming brightness of Bliss.

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: They will be served the choicest wine, sealed

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: They will be given pure wine to drink, which is kept preserved, sealed.

25.author: Arberry
25.text: as they are given to drink of a wine sealed

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: They will be given to drink of pure wine, sealed.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: They will be given to drink pure sealed wine.

25.author: Itani
25.text: They will be given to drink a sealed wine.

25.author: Maududi
25.text: They will be served a drink of the finest sealed wine,

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: They will be given to drink of pure sealed Rahiq.

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: They are given to drink of a pure wine, sealed,

25.author: Qarai
25.text: They will be served with a sealed pure wine,

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: They shall be given to drink of a wine that is sealed,

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: They will be given to drink [pure] wine [which was] sealed.

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: They will be given pure wine out of sealed containers

25.author: Shakir
25.text: They are made to quaff of a pure drink that is sealed (to others).

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: They will be given a drink of pure wine, sealed,

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: Their thirst will be slaked with Pure Wine sealed:

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: With a sealing of musk, which those who aspire for the best should desire, --

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: Its seal is upon musk; and for this should those who crave be eager.

26.author: Arberry
26.text: whose seal is musk -- so after that let the strivers strive --

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: The seal thereof will be of musk and to this end let the aspirers aspire -

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: The last thereof (that wine) will be the smell of musk, and for this let (all) those strive who want to strive (i.e. hasten earnestly to the obedience of Allah).

26.author: Itani
26.text: Whose seal is musk—this is what competitors should compete for.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: whose seal is musk – so let all aspirants aspire after that –

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: Sealed with musk, and for this let those strive who want to strive.

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: Whose seal is musk - for this let (all) those strive who strive for bliss -

26.author: Qarai
26.text: whose seal is musk—for such let the viers vie—

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: its seal is musk, for this let the competitors compete;

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: The last of it is musk. So for this let the competitors compete.

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: which have the fragrance of musk. This is the kind of place for which one should really aspire.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: The sealing of it is (with) musk; and for that let the aspirers aspire.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: its seal will be of musk -- for this let the aspirants aspire --

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: The seal thereof will be Musk: And for this let those aspire, who have aspirations:

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Khitamuhu miskun wafee thalika falyatanafasi almutanafisoona

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: Blended with the water of Tasnim, (heights ultimate of evolution),

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: And it is mixed with Tasneem.

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: And admixture thereof will be Water of Tasnim:

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: It (that wine) will be mixed with Tasnim.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: a wine whose mixture is Tasnim,

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: It will be mixed with Tasnim:

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: And mixed with water of Tasnim,

27.author: Qarai
27.text: and whose seasoning is from Tasneem,

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: And its mixture is of Tasneem,

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: With the wine is a drink from Tasnim,

27.author: Shakir
27.text: And the admixture of it is a water of Tasnim,

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: a wine tempered with the waters of Tasnim,

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: With it will be (given) a mixture of Tasnim:

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: A fountain from which only they who are honoured drink.

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: The spring from which drink the ones close to Allah.

28.author: Arberry
28.text: a fountain at which do drink those brought nigh.

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: A fountain whereof will drink those brought nigh.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: A spring whereof drink those nearest to Allah.

28.author: Itani
28.text: A spring from which those brought near drink.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: a fountain at which the chosen ones shall drink.

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: A spring whereof drink those nearest to Allah.

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: A spring whence those brought near (to Allah) drink.

28.author: Qarai
28.text: a spring where those brought near [to Allah] drink.

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: a fountain at which those brought near (to their Lord) drink.

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: A spring from which those near [to Allah] drink.

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: a spring, the nearest ones to God will drink from it.

28.author: Shakir
28.text: A fountain from which drink they who are drawn near (to Allah).

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: a spring at which those drawn close to God will drink.

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: A spring, from (the waters) whereof drink those Nearest to Allah.

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: The sinners indeed laughed at believers

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: Indeed the guilty used to laugh at the believers.

29.author: Arberry
29.text: Behold, the sinners were laughing at the believers,

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: Veriiy those who have sinned were wont at those who had believed to laugh,

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: Verily! (During the worldly life) those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed.

29.author: Itani
29.text: Those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed.

29.author: Maududi
29.text: Behold, the wicked were wont to laugh at the believers:

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: Verily, those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed.

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: Lo! the guilty used to laugh at those who believed,

29.author: Qarai
29.text: Indeed the guilty used to laugh at the faithful,

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: The sinners laughed at the believers

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: Indeed, those who committed crimes used to laugh at those who believed.

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: The sinners had been laughing at the believers.

29.author: Shakir
29.text: Surely they who are guilty used to laugh at those who believe.

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: The wicked used to laugh at the believers --

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: Those in sin used to laugh at those who believed,

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Inna allatheena ajramoo kanoo mina allatheena amanoo yadhakoona

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: And winked at one another as they passed by them;

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: And when the believers used to pass by, they used to gesture at each other with their eyes.

30.author: Arberry
30.text: when them passed them by winking at one another,

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: And, when they passed them, to wink at each other,

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: And whenever they passed by them, used to wink one to another (in mockery);

30.author: Itani
30.text: And when they passed by them, they would wink at one another.

30.author: Maududi
30.text: when they passed by them they winked,

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: And, whenever they passed by them, used to wink one to another.

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: And wink one to another when they passed them;

30.author: Qarai
30.text: and when they passed them by they would wink at each other,

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: and winked at one another as they passed them by.

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: And when they passed by them, they would exchange derisive glances.

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: When passing by them, they would wink at one another

30.author: Shakir
30.text: And when they passed by them, they winked at one another.

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: when they passed by them, they would wink at one another;

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: And whenever they passed by them, used to wink at each other (in mockery);

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: And when they went back to their people turned to make fun of them;

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: And whilst returning to their homes, they used to return rejoicing.

31.author: Arberry
31.text: and when they returned to their people they returned blithely,

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: And when they returned to their household they returned jesting.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: And when they returned to their own people, they would return jesting;

31.author: Itani
31.text: And when they went back to their families, they would go back exulting.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: and when they went back to their families, they went back jesting,

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: And when they returned to their own people, they would return jesting;

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: And when they returned to their own folk, they returned jesting;

31.author: Qarai
31.text: and when they returned to their folks they would return amused,

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: When they returned to their people they returned jesting;

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: And when they returned to their people, they would return jesting.

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: and, on returning to their people, boast about what they had done.

31.author: Shakir
31.text: And when they returned to their own followers they returned exulting.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: and when they returned to their own people, they would speak of them jestingly;

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: And when they returned to their own people, they would return jesting;

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: Waitha inqalaboo ila ahlihimu inqalaboo fakiheena

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: And when they saw them, they said: "They have indeed gone astray."

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: And upon seeing the Muslims, they used to say, “Indeed they have gone astray.”

32.author: Arberry
32.text: and when they saw them they said, 'Lo, these men are astray!'

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: And when they saw them, they said: verily these are the strayed ones.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: And when they saw them, they said: "Verily! These have indeed gone astray!"

32.author: Itani
32.text: And if they saw them, they would say, “These people are lost.”

32.author: Maududi
32.text: and when they saw the believers, they said: “Lo! These are the erring ones”;

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: And when they saw them, they said: "Verily, these have indeed gone astray!"

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: And when they saw them they said: Lo! these have gone astray.

32.author: Qarai
32.text: and when they saw them they would say, ‘Indeed those are the astray!’

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: and when they saw them said: 'These are they who are astray'

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: And when they saw them, they would say, "Indeed, those are truly lost."

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: On seeing the believers, they would say, "These people have gone astray".

32.author: Shakir
32.text: And when they saw them, they said: Most surely these are in error;

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: and when they saw them, they said [scornfully], "These men have surely gone astray,"

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: And whenever they saw them, they would say, "Behold! These are the people truly astray!"

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Waitha raawhum qaloo inna haolai ladalloona

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: But they were not Sent to be guardians over them!

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: Whereas they have not at all been sent as guardians over them.

33.author: Arberry
33.text: Yet they were not sent as watchers over them.

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: Whereas they were not sent over them as watchers.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: But they (disbelievers, sinners) had not been sent as watchers over them (the believers).

33.author: Itani
33.text: Yet they were not sent as guardians over them.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: (they said so although) they had not been appointed watchers over them.

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: But they were not sent as watchers over them.

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: Yet they were not sent as guardians over them.

33.author: Qarai
33.text: Though they were not sent to watch over them.

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: Yet they were not sent to be their guardians.

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: But they had not been sent as guardians over them.

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: No one has appointed them to watch over the believers.

33.author: Shakir
33.text: And they were not sent to be keepers over them.

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: though they were not sent to be their keepers --

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: But they had not been sent as keepers over them!

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: So, the believers will laugh at the infidels on that day,

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: So this day it is the believers who laugh at the disbelievers.

34.author: Arberry
34.text: So today the believers are laughing at the unbelievers,

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: Wherefore Today, those who believed at the infidels are laughing,

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: But this Day (the Day of Resurrection) those who believe will laugh at the disbelievers

34.author: Itani
34.text: But on that Day, those who believed will laugh at the unbelievers.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: But today the believers are laughing at the unbelievers;

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: But this Day those who believe will laugh at the disbelievers

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: This day it is those who believe who have the laugh of disbelievers,

34.author: Qarai
34.text: So today the faithful will laugh at the faithless,

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: But on this Day the believers will laugh at the unbelievers

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: So Today those who believed are laughing at the disbelievers,

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: On the Day of Judgment, the believers will laugh at the disbelievers

34.author: Shakir
34.text: So today those who believe shall laugh at the unbelievers;

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: so today those who believe shall [be able to] laugh at those who denied the truth

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: But on this Day the Believers will laugh at the Unbelievers:

34.author: Transliteration
34.text: Faalyawma allatheena amanoo mina alkuffari yadhakoona

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: Regarding them from their cushioned seats.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: seated upon their couches, they are looking around.

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: as they (recline) upon their couches and gaze (around them).

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: while reclining on couches and reviewing (the bounties given to them).

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: as they sit on couches, gazing around.

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: On Thrones (of Dignity) they will command (a sight) (of all things).

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: Will not the infidels pay for what they had done?

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: Did not the disbelievers get repaid for what they used to do?

36.author: Arberry
36.text: Have the unbelievers been rewarded what they were doing?

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: The infidels have indeed been rewarded for that which they had been doing.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: Are not the disbelievers paid (fully) for what they used to do?

36.author: Itani
36.text: Have the unbelievers been repaid for what they used to do?

36.author: Maududi
36.text: Have the unbelievers been duly rewarded for their deeds?

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: Are not the disbelievers paid for what they used to do

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: Are not the disbelievers paid for what they used to do?

36.author: Qarai
36.text: Have the faithless been requited for what they used to do?

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: Have the unbelievers been rewarded for what they did?

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: Have the disbelievers [not] been rewarded [this Day] for what they used to do?

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: Will not the disbelievers then be duly recompensed for their laughing at the believers?

36.author: Shakir
36.text: Surely the disbelievers are rewarded as they did.

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: Have those who deny the truth [not] been paid back for their deeds?

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: Will not the Unbelievers have been paid back for what they did?

36.author: Transliteration
36.text: Hal thuwwiba alkuffaru ma kanoo yafAAaloona
